flares.note: End time is cut off by a small, but visually distinct in GOES SUVI 131, separate C3.2 peak from AR 14401 (N23E65) starting at 2026-03-23T00:24Z that peaks at 2026-03-23T00:29Z.
Double-peaked flare preceding a C3.7 flare peaking at 2026-03-25T00:30Z.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the West by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-19T01:26Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 in later frames. This event is not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 and is obscured by Earthshine in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is likely some minor field line movement seen along or beyond the West limb in GOES SUVI 195 beginning at approx. 2026-03-19T00:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of W90 due to source near the limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate or STEREO A EUVI imagery to observe a source beyond the West limb.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-19T04:36Z, as well as by STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. The source of this CME is a faint region of dimming beginning at approx. 2026-03-19T03:56Z located at approx. S40E15 as seen in GOES SUVI 195. This event was likely defected due to its proximity to a coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Very narrow CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-19T07:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. There is no clear source seen on the disk for this event, as it likely originated near the North limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Slow, streamer blowout-like CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-19T20:36Z. It is also seen the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 and to the NNE in STEREO A COR2. It is also seen to the NE in GOES CCOR-1 but is mostly obscured by the pylon. The source is possibly related to a large but slow filament eruption over the NE limb beginning around 2026-03-19T18:23Z best seen in GOES SUVI 304. The filament can be seen rising a few hours prior to the eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement relies heavily on later frames in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as there appears to be some acceleration as the CME propagates farther from the Sun.

cmes.note: Very faint front first seen to the SW part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-03-20T06:36Z. This front is only seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and SOHO LASCO C3. The source is possibly related to a small eruption near the SW limb best seen in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-03-20T07:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on probable source location on the SW limb around W90.

cmes.note: Very faint CME first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-20T07:48Z. It is also seen for a couple frames to the W in SOHO LASCO C3 but is not seen by STEREO A COR2 or GOES CCOR-1. There are a plethora of very small eruptions across the disk, none are definitive source candidates.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3.

cmes.note: This outflow-like CME is visible to the NE following the CME first seen to the NE at 2026-03-20T11:24Z. This CME also overlaps the CME first seen to the NE at 2026-03T20T11:24Z. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-03-20T11:24Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3, to the NNE in STEREO A COR2. It is also visible to the NE in GOES CCOR-1 but is partially obscured by the pylon. The source is most likely an eruption seen near the NE limb starting around 2026-03-20T10:20Z best seen in GOES SUVI 195 as an opening of field lines.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and on visible source location near NE limb.

cmes.note: CME visible to the west in STEREO A COR2 and as a partial halo in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Overlaps in the field of view with CME: 2026-03-20T12:38Z. The source is likely farsided, and believed to likely be a separate CME from CME: 2026-03-20T12:38Z based on its appearance in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 which have the clearest view of the separate features between CME: 2026-03-20T12:23Z and CME: 2026-03-20T12:38Z.
Preliminary measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Partial halo CME visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The CME is also visible to the west in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely farsided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary analysis based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Outflow-like CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting around 2026-03-20T17:00Z. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery.

cmes.note: Very minor and faint CME seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Fully covered by the evening data gap in STEREO A COR2. No source for it has been found in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A and no source for it has been found in EUV imagery, so only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not observed in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. No direct source for this front is observed, so it may likely be far-sided over the NE limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 plane-of-sky due to no available STEREO A COR2 or source visibility for triangulation.

cmes.note: Faint, fuzzy and relatively narrow front seen to the ENE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The CME is brighter and seen to the ENE in STEREO A COR2, but obscured by Earthshine in GOES CCOR-1. A possible source could be a very minor eruption indicated by very minor faint post-eruptive arcades followed by dimming seen in GOES SUVI 195 centered around ~N10E65 starting at 2026-03-21T01:52Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate, somewhat speculative analysis based on the fitting of the fuzzy faint front in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.

cmes.note: Faint CME with a fuzzy front seen to the ENE in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 and possibly seen very faintly in STEREO A COR2, but the front in COR2A is too fuzzy to use in analysis. No clear source has been found for this event in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is seen clearly in SOHO LASCO only, with the potential front too faint/fuzzy in STEREO A COR2. No clear source is seen in coronagraphs. Only plane-of-sky analysis is definitive.

cmes.note: Very faint, fuzzy front to the ENE in SOHO LASCO C2, dissipating after 3-4 early timestamps, also very faintly seen in SOHO LASCO C3 and not seen in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. No clear source found for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and no clear source is found for it in EUV imagery.

cmes.note: CME faintly seen to the ENE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and possibly also seen super faintly in STEREO A COR2. Possibly far-sided source could be indicated by moving field lines seen after 2026-03-21T08:00Z on/close to the E limb in GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very speculative analysis using triangulation and based on the fitting of two very faint fronts in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A coronagraphs.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1, not visible in LASCO C3 in real time due to a downlink gap. Not observed in available STEREO A COR2 imagery likely due to being directed towards STEREO A. The source is likely a dimming region centered around S12W45 slightly N of AR 14392, observed in GOES SUVI 195 starting around 2026-03-21T15:08Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D analysis with one perspective, using SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1, as STEREO A COR2 does not observe the CME front.

cmes.note: Semi-faint CME visible to the S/SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and to the SE in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source of this CME appears to be a slow moving/opening of field lines and dimming/brightening in the SE quadrant of the Earth-facing disk starting around 2026-03-21T18:30Z as seen in GOES SUVI 195, 304, and 284 centered around S45E30. There is a second eruption visible near S60W50 which may contribute to overlapping features in the outflowing part at the back of the CME starting around 2026-03-22T00:30Z as seen in GOES SUVI 304 and 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery of the possible shock front of this CME. It is unclear if this is a shock or a fainter CME following the same path ahead of the brighter leading edge measurement.

Parameters based on best fit of brighter portion of CME as seen in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is some uncertainty with this CME as to whether this is a separate event or associated with the same eruption.

cmes.note: This somewhat asymmetric bright CME is visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph and for a couple of frames before the usual overnight data gap in STEREO A COR2. Its source is a significant filament eruption with associated brightening/dimming starting around 2026-03-22T15:30Z, followed by high-rising post-eruptive arcades, as seen in GOES SUVI 304/284/195/131 centered around S20E15, south of Active Region 14398, and seen as ejecta and opening of field lines in STEREO A EUV 195/403 on/close to the SE limb. It appears that there may be some SE deflection/directionality to the CME as it erupts and progresses outward in the GOES SUVI 284 field of view. There is a bright bulk front and a wider faint shock front seen in all three coronagraphs.

cmeAnalyses.note: Final analysis of this CME with later imagery from SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 and with a few early timestamps of STEREO A COR2 before the routine evening data gap.

Analysis of the fainter wider shock front based on the fit with the imagery from SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs, including the early imagery from STEREO A COR2 before the usual evening data gap and fully available SOHO LASCO imagery.
PRELIMINARY MEASUREMENT USING FIRST FEW INITIAL FRAMES. Parameters based on best fit using three frames of SOHO LASCO C2 imagery and four frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint loop CME seen in difference imagery of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but covered by a daily data gap in STEREO A COR2. No source was found for it in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery and no source has been found for it in EUV imagery. Only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

cmes.note: Faint CME seen to the W in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 imagery but in STEREO A COR2 it is covered by the daily data gap. No source for it has been found in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery and no source has been found for it in EUV imagery. Only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

cmes.note: CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, also W in STEREO A COR2 after a data gap ends at 2026-03-23T03:38Z. The source is a wide eruption from AR 14392 (S17W65), associated with a C1.5 flare peaking at 2026-03-22T23:09Z as observed in GOES SUVI 131. A small filament eruption near AR 14392 begins at 2026-03-22T22:50Z in GOES SUVI 304, setting off a wider rising and breaking of field lines over AR 14392 and likely extending around the west limb, starting around 2026-03-22T23:34Z in GOES SUVI 171/195/284. This is followed by a post-eruptive arcade over AR 14392 observed in GOES SUVI 171/195/284/304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulated bulk analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. Due to the distorted lobed shape of the CME front, this analysis best captures the faster northern leading edge of the CME front using the full width of the bright bulk material. The CME front is further distorted in STEREO A COR2's field-of-view, but able to be captured good enough to track with the other coronagraphs.

SHOCK analysis using only SOHO LASCO C3, as the shock front is not resolvable in STEREO A COR2 imagery potentially due to faintness, being directed towards the spacecraft, and/or the COR2A data gap ending at 2026-03-23T03:38Z. The longitude could vary up to W55-W85, the source longitude of W65 is used as a best approximation with available imagery.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and as a partial halo to the S/SW in STEREO A COR2. This CME immediately follows the larger CME 2026-03-22T23:24Z, and it is very likely outflow or a streamer blowout following that eruption from AR 14392 (S17W65), corroborated via coronagraph triangulation in the swpc_cat tool. The field line loops over AR 14392, which initially began to erupt around 2026-03-22T23:34Z in GOES SUVI 171/195/284, seen as a second movement of field lines starting around 2026-03-23T01:31Z. That first eruption is associated with a C1.5 flare as observed in GOES SUVI 131, which is not linked to this second CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, using later frames only due to observed acceleration when including early frames. The CME shape is very distorted in STEREO A COR2 compared to SOHO LASCO, appearing much wider. Using STEREO A COR2's width, the leading edge still tracks very well between COR2 and C3.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint, jet-like CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SW only in GOES CCOR-1. SOHO LASCO is in a downlink gap, and the CME may be too faint or directed towards STEREO A COR2. No distinct source for this CME is observed, but based on its direction and recent eruptions it may be an outflow from AR 14392 following significant CMEs 2026-03-22T23:24Z and 2026-03-23T02:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: GOES CCOR-1 only plane-of-sky due to no other coronagraphs observing this front in real-time and unobserved source.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the West by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-03-24T03:53 following the end of a data gap which began at 2026-03-23T20:23Z. This CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1, as it is likely completely far sided with respect to the Sun-Earth line. This event is also quite faint in STERO A COR2 and is therefore only visible for a few frames. Due to the data gap in STEREO A and the far sided nature of the CME, no clear source is seen for this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A plane-of-sky using a longitude of W143.8 due to lack of visible source and usable coronagraph imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-24T12:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a very small eruption seen beginning at approx. 2026-03-24T11:12Z located at approx. S40W35 as seen in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME seen to the NE in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 in real-time due to a data gap starting at 2026-03-24T13:53Z. The source is a C7.2 flare starting at 2026-03-24T17:18Z as seen in GOES SUVI 131 imagery. Opening field lines and post-eruptive arcades are also visible near the NE limb in GOES SUVI 171/195/284/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement with additional coronagraph imagery. Measurement based on the source location. The longitude is approximate based on location near the east limb.
Measurement based on the source location. The longitude is approximate due to the source location near the east limb.

cmes.note: CME visible to the southwest in GOES CCOR-1 and in limited available SOHO LASCO C3 imagery in real-time during a data downlink period. Not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting at 2026-03-24T13:53Z. The source is a C2.9 flare and associated eruption from AR 14400 (approximately S14W52) starting at 2026-03-24T23:52Z as seen in GOES SUVI 195/284 with rapidly opening field lines and possible EUV wave.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated parameters based on SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery with matching to possible features in STEREO A COR2 following the data gap. Possible deflection within the FOV due to nearby CH.
Preliminary measurement based on the source location using 3 frames of available GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Blocked in CCOR-1 by Earth-shine. No clear source but appears to be within the outflow of the CME: 2026-03-24T20:15Z (CCOR-1).

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A, and possibly in GOES CCOR-1 following the Earth-shine event. This CME follows in the same path of the CMEs: 2026-03-24T20:15Z (CCOR-1) and 2026-03-25T03:36Z (C2). No clear source but appears to be within the outflow of the aforementioned CMEs.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source appears to be moving/opening field lines just beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-03-25T07:16Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME appears to overlap the CME first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-03-25T09:36Z. The source of this CME appears to be moving/opening field lines visible beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 09:00Z, overlaps source movement from CME: 2026-03-25T09:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Bright and slow CME visible to the SE in GOES CCOR-1, and for a frame in SOHO LASCO C2 before a data gap starting at 2026-03-25T12:36Z. It is also visible due S in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a filament eruption visible along/just beyond the SE limb in GOES SUVI 304 with associated moving/opening field lines in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-03-25T07:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
PRELIMINARY parameters based on best fit between one frame of SOHO LASCO C2 and multiple frames in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
